rulings.Case Title: YING WANG VS. ANTHONY GO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (RE: PLAINTIFF YING WANG)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (RE: PLAINTIFF YING WANG) is OFF CALENDAR, Mandatory form MC-052 not on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE BERNARD CLEMENTS, JR., AN INDIVIDUAL VS. MILOUS JAMES IVORY, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to all references to attorney's fees for Plaintiff to allege facts showing entitled for recovery of fees (statute, contract, etc) and/or re-allege as request for consequential damaged. Motion is otherwise DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the portions of the complaint subject to this motion (not discussed above) are improper, irrelevant or false. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT COSGAYA, TRUSTEE OF THE ROBERTO COSGAYA VS. MARIA CLAUDIO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 11, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith the requisite relationship with the defendant (landlord/tenant; licensor/licensee; employer/employee); the service and expiration of the operative notice terminating the relationship whether TPA 2019 applies and has been complied with; whether SFRO applies and has been complied with. The Court Notes that the form UD complaint is not mandatory and pleading-format complaint maybe used if more allegations are required for clarity than contemplated by a form complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
